methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $370M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Boston Metal, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Donald Sadoway and Antoine Allanore, has raised $370M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on two primary methods triangulated against a comparable set of 9 peer companies (1 public, 8 private), including same-stage peers KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

The stage-progression method, applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to the $370M total raised proxy, implies a valuation of $629M. The peer-median method across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B. These approaches reflect materially different underlying assumptions about Boston Metal's relative progress and market positioning within the climate tech cohort.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $535M to $1.1B, with a median of $814M. The 100+ basis point spread between the low and high reflects genuine uncertainty around whether Boston Metal's development trajectory and technical risk profile align more closely with the stage-progression baseline or with the higher multiples commanded by leading peers. The key uncertainty is the company's path to commercial scale and unit economics validation relative to the peer set.
